title: Click a source → Properties Panel updates
summary: ParaView's central signal cascade. One click in the Pipeline Browser fans out to every panel that cares — via a single global singleton (pqActiveObjects) emitting Qt signals — rather than direct wiring.

steps.note: Tree selection is a thin wrapper over pqPipelineModel — clicks become signals, not direct method calls.
Tree click handler funnels into the global active-object singleton — the single source of truth for 'what is selected right now'.
Properties Panel, Color Editor, Info Panel, View Frame buttons all rebind off this signal. No direct A → B wiring.
Panel clears previous editors and rebuilds for the new selection.
Walk every property on the proxy — type + domain drive which Qt widget to create.

steps.call: per-property widget created and added to layout
steps.passes: QWidget* (slider, combo, line edit, file picker, …)
steps.note: Property domain determines the editor: DoubleRangeDomain → slider, EnumerationDomain → combo, ArrayListDomain → array picker.

title: Toggle eye icon on pipeline node → actor hides
summary: Visibility is a property on the representation, not a UI flag. Clicking the eye toggles a typed property and the view re-renders through the standard pipeline.

steps.note: Model handles the click on column 0 (icon) rather than delegating to the tree's selection.

steps.passes: repr: pqDataRepresentation*, view: pqView*, visible: bool
steps.note: Policy object decides whether a representation should be created on-the-fly, or just toggled.
It's just a property change. Same mechanism as every other property edit.
All accumulated property changes are flushed to the VTK object in one call.
View asks each visible representation to draw — the hidden one is skipped because its actor's visibility is now 0.

title: Edit property → Apply → pipeline updates → view re-renders
summary: The Apply button is a transaction. Edits accumulate as 'unchecked values' on properties — the GUI can preview without committing. One click commits them all and propagates through the DAG.

steps.call: pqProxyWidget: user types in QLineEdit → SetUncheckedValue
steps.note: Unchecked = previewable but not yet pushed. Domains catch invalid values before commit.
Apply button is bound to this signal — it enables on any pending change, disables after apply.

steps.passes: for each modified proxy: SetUncheckedValue → SetValue
steps.note: Behavior owns the apply cycle. Auto-Apply is just this behavior firing on every changeAvailable() instead of on button click.

steps.passes: serialized property → C++ setter calls on VTK object
steps.note: Single round-trip pushes all dirty properties at once.
Standard VTK observer pattern. ParaView listens for this on every source.
Pipeline DAG is walked — anything that consumes the modified source's output port invalidates.
View asks reps for fresh data; reps ask their source to update; source executes; new data flows back; actor renders.

title: Change color array → transfer function rebinds → scalar bar updates
summary: Coloring is a property of the representation, not the source. A separate transfer-function proxy holds the colormap — multiple representations can share or override it.

steps.note: Widget shows the input's available arrays — driven by domain query on the representation's ColorArrayName property.
Color array is just another typed property on the representation proxy.
Transfer functions are global, keyed by array name — two reps of 'Pressure' share the same LUT by default.
Mapper now uses the shared LUT. Editing it once recolors every rep that points at it.
Editor dock follows the active rep automatically — see flow #1 for the cascade.

steps.passes: scalar bar with title, ticks, range
steps.note: Scalar bar is a VTK actor inside the render window, not a Qt widget. Survives screenshots and exports.

title: VTK warning fires → routed to Output dock
summary: Every vtkWarningMacro, vtkErrorMacro, Python exception, and qWarning is captured by a single message handler and routed (thread-safe) to one dock — color-coded by severity.

steps.note: VTK's standard message macros all funnel into vtkOutputWindow — the singleton that ParaView overrides.

steps.call: vtkPVOutputWindow forwards to Qt via callback
steps.note: ParaView replaces the default output window at startup. The replacement bridges into Qt's message system.
Captures qDebug/qWarning/qCritical from Qt AND from the VTK bridge. Single funnel for the whole app.

steps.call: MessageHandler emits Qt signal — thread-safe routing to UI thread
steps.note: Critical: handler may fire from a worker thread. Emitting a signal Qt-marshals to the UI thread.
Red = error, yellow = warning, gray = info. Lets users see severity at a glance.
Low-bandwidth feedback. User sees '3 warnings' badge; click raises the dock.

title: Click Split → multi-view re-tiles
summary: View frame chrome is a thin QToolBar with registered actions. Splits are server-side: the Qt side just reacts to a layout proxy change.

steps.note: Title-bar buttons are QActions registered via addTitleBarAction — plugins use the same mechanism.
Qt widget owns no layout state — it just delegates to the server-side layout proxy.

steps.call: vtkSMViewLayoutProxy mutates cell tree, creates new empty cell
steps.note: Logical layout = a binary tree of cells. Splits add a new branch.

steps.call: ProxyEvent fires; pqMultiViewWidget observer triggers reload()
steps.note: Qt walks the new layout tree and rebuilds the QSplitter hierarchy to match. Authoritative state is on the server side.

steps.call: new pqViewFrame created and inserted at new cell
steps.note: Each frame is independent — its own toolbar, its own active state. Border color tracks which is active.

title: Open Settings → tabs auto-generated from proxies
summary: Settings are proxies. The dialog walks the 'settings' proxy group and builds a tab per proxy — zero per-option UI code. Search filters across all tabs.

steps.note: Reaction pattern — every action wraps a QAction with a thin handler class.
Dialog opens, then asks the proxy manager 'who is in the settings group?'
Settings proxies are registered the same way as filter proxies — XML-defined.

steps.call: for each proxy: create QTabWidget tab containing pqProxyWidget
steps.note: Same proxy widget as the Properties Panel. One implementation, two contexts.
Global search hides non-matching properties. Users find any option without knowing which tab it's on.

steps.call: OK → apply changes; pqSettings persists to disk
steps.note: Restart-required settings are flagged; the dialog warns the user before closing.

title: Apply Contour to Sphere → full DAG flow
summary: The four-role pipeline in motion. A filter is a SourceProxy that consumes another SourceProxy's output. The view doesn't see data — it sees representations.

steps.note: Proxy types are defined in XML (Remoting/Application/Resources/filters_filterscore.xml). No C++ per new filter.
This is the edge in the pipeline DAG — typed, validated against producer's output data type.
Apply commits all unchecked property values across all modified proxies.

steps.passes: execute filter, expose new output port metadata
steps.note: UpdatePipelineInformation runs only metadata (array names, bounds), not the heavy filter. Drives the GUI's array pickers.
Representation is a separate proxy from the source. One source can feed multiple representations (wireframe + solid).
View asks each visible rep for its actor; reps run the upstream pipeline; data flows into mappers; actor appears.

title: File → Save State → .pvsm written
summary: State save is reflection on the proxy graph. Every property serializes itself; every connection writes itself. The file is XML, human-readable, version-resilient.

steps.note: Reaction wraps the menu action. Same pattern as every other action in the app.
Walks every registered proxy in the current session.
Proxy emits its own XML. New proxy types need zero serialization code — they inherit it.
Each property type knows how to serialize itself. Input properties write proxy id refs; vectors write per-element.

steps.call: ProxyCollection entries — proxy registration, names, logs
steps.note: Captures user-friendly names and grouping (sources, views, representations…).
Loading reverses the same path: parse XML → instantiate proxies → set properties → reconnect inputs.
